summary: Repository-wide formatting pass produced by `make format`, touching 46 files across runtime, Dolphin, fighter, ground/stage, item, menu, lb, and baselib code. The changes are almost entirely mechanical clang-format output: pointer-star placement, spacing around operators and casts, include ordering, line wrapping, brace placement, comment alignment, and blank-line normalization. The PR body explicitly asks contributors to ensure clang-format is reading the repo `.clang-format` and to propose formatting behavior changes there rather than hand-formatting individual files.
what_changed: Ran the repository formatter over 46 files, with 1393 added and 1134 deleted lines but no evidence in the provided slice of intentional semantic changes.
Normalized macro spacing around `- 1` in alignment/offset helpers such as `ALIGN`, `OFFSET`, `TRUNC`, and `ROUND` in Runtime, CARD, OSAlloc, and OSArena code.

Applied project pointer-style formatting such as `HSD_GObj* gobj` and `f32* arg1` instead of `HSD_GObj *gobj` or `f32 *arg1`, including large prototype blocks in `grflatzone.h` and declarations in `grkongo.c`.
Rewrapped long function calls and declarations, for example `lbRefract_80022998`, `ftMaterial_800BF534`, and several Kongo stage expressions.
Normalized include ordering and grouping in files such as `ftLk_SpecialN.c` and `grflatzone.h`.
Collapsed or expanded braces and blank lines according to formatter output, including empty functions in `grkongo.c` and multi-condition `if` formatting in `ftaction.c`.
Formatted struct member comments in `src/melee/ft/types.h`, reducing padded alignment in comments for `FtCmdState`.
Added/normalized a trailing blank line in `src/dolphin/ax/AXVPB.c`, which was an empty file before the formatting pass.
smart_moves: The PR kept the scope explicitly mechanical by using `make format`, making it easy to review as a style normalization rather than a decomp logic change.
The author pointed contributors at the repository `.clang-format` as the source of truth, preventing local clang-format defaults from creating noisy churn.
The PR notes that desired formatting behavior should be changed by PRing `.clang-format`, which centralizes style debates and avoids one-off formatting exceptions.
Running the formatter across generated/noisy areas like headers, item code, and stage code reduces future diff noise for real matching work in those files.
decomp_lessons: For decomp PRs, run `make format` before review to avoid mixing matching changes with mechanical style changes.
Do not infer behavior from formatting-only diffs: pointer-style changes, line wrapping, include sorting, and macro whitespace are not evidence of semantic decomp progress by themselves.
Keep formatting changes separate from symbol recovery or matching fixes when possible; this PR demonstrates a dedicated formatting cleanup that future PRs can avoid repeating.
When clang-format output looks wrong locally, verify that the tool is reading the repository `.clang-format` rather than changing individual files by hand.
Formatting large prototype blocks in headers can create broad churn, so future decomp work should avoid manual alignment that the formatter will later remove.
naming_conventions: Formatted C pointer stars attach to the type/name style used by the formatter, e.g. `HSD_GObj*`, `Ground_GObj*`, `Vec3*`, `HSD_JObj*`, and `StageCallbacks*`.
Function and symbol names were preserved; examples in the slice include address-based decomp names such as `grKongo_801D523C`, `grFlatzone_80216F48`, `ftMaterial_800BF534`, and `ftCo_80094098`.
Extern/static comments were normalized but not renamed, e.g. `void mpLib_800580AC(s32); /* extern */` and `int fn_801D8134(...); /* static */`.
Struct field placeholder names and comments were preserved while spacing was normalized, e.g. `float x0; // timer`, `float x4; // frame`, and `char* x8; // script`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No explicit matching tactic or assembly workaround was introduced in the provided evidence; the diff is a mechanical formatter run.

Formatter changes affected inline assembly macro whitespace in `gmmain.c` around `MTSPR`, illustrating that asm-containing macros can be touched by `make format` and should be checked for unintended readability churn.
Arithmetic macro formatting such as `((size) - 1)` and cast spacing such as `(f32) M_TAU` were normalized without changing the apparent expressions.
Because this is formatting-only, future matching PRs should not reuse it as evidence for control-flow, register-allocation, or type-correction tactics.
review_feedback: No GitHub review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text asks `@thefoxcam` to make sure clang-format is pulling configuration from `.clang-format`.
The PR text says formatting behavior changes should be proposed by editing `.clang-format`.
The PR text also notes contributors can run `make format` if Make is configured locally.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs near #1407 that introduced the formatting drift this PR corrected, especially by the mentioned contributor.
Search for later PRs modifying `.clang-format` to understand whether any formatting behavior changed after this cleanup.
Check whether subsequent matching PRs in `grkongo.c`, `it_266F.c`, `itlinkhookshot.c`, or `mncharsel.c` relied on this formatting cleanup to reduce diff noise.
Look for CI or contributor documentation updates that require `make format` before submitting decomp PRs.
